medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for anxiety (usually evenings)
Trazodone 50 mg at bedtime as needed for sleep
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for back pain
Fluticasone nasal spray daily during allergy season
health.mental_health: Anxiety and depressive symptoms have worsened over the past several months due to ongoing workplace harassment; he reports rumination before shifts, occasional panic-like symptoms when his supervisor texts, and difficulty sleeping. He is considering legal action and worries about retaliation and job loss, which feeds into his mood and concentration problems.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (Feb 2026) for sleep issues and medication refill; discussed work stress
Telehealth therapy intake (Jan 2026) for anxiety/depression; started weekly sessions
Urgent care visit (Dec 2025) for acute back strain after lifting an air handler

finance.debt: Auto loan with about 3 years remaining and revolving credit card debt that has grown over the last 6 months due to reduced overtime and paying out-of-pocket for therapy copays; he is also setting aside money for a legal consultation retainer.
investments: Small emergency savings in a credit union account
recent_transactions: Initial consultation fee paid to an employment attorney in Tampa
Pharmacy copays for sertraline and trazodone
Purchase of a digital voice recorder and cloud storage subscription for documenting incidents

relationships: Spouse: Priya Patel (works part-time in retail; supportive but worried about finances)
Adult daughter: Anaya Patel (22, lives in Orlando; encourages him to report harassment)
Close friend/co-worker: Luis Herrera (occasionally rides along; has witnessed some incidents)
Older sister: Nneka Okafor (lives out of state; urges him to protect his mental health)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive use of Facebook and YouTube; follows local weather and home-repair pages. He avoids posting about work and has tightened privacy settings after fearing coworkers might monitor him.
private_conversations: Discussing whether to file an EEOC complaint and what evidence he needs
Talking with his spouse about retaliation fears and whether they can afford a lawyer
Texting a trusted coworker about specific harassment incidents and witnesses
Asking his therapist how to cope with panic symptoms triggered by his supervisor

schedule.work_hours: Typically Mon–Fri 7:00am–3:30pm with rotating on-call weekends; overtime increases during summer heat waves
regular_appointments: Weekly telehealth therapy session on Wednesdays at 6:30pm
Monthly primary care check-in or medication refill call (varies by month)
Physical therapy home exercises 3x/week for back pain (self-directed)
upcoming_events: Follow-up meeting with employment attorney (next Tuesday at 4:30pm)
HVAC safety training refresh at the shop (Friday morning)
Daughter visiting from Orlando for a weekend in two weeks
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:30am, quick coffee and light breakfast, then drives to the shop to load parts and dispatch. He tries to get in a short walk after work, eats dinner with his spouse, and spends late evenings documenting workplace incidents and reviewing messages before attempting sleep.

professional.company: Mid-sized residential and light commercial HVAC contractor serving Hillsborough and Pinellas counties; non-union shop with a central dispatch office in Tampa
projects: Summer readiness tune-ups for condo associations along Bayshore Blvd
Replacing a rooftop package unit for a small strip-mall clinic in Carrollwood
Warranty compressor swap for a new-build home in Riverview
On-call rotation planning for peak season
professional.performance_issues: Since the harassment escalated, he has had trouble concentrating, double-checks paperwork excessively, and has requested fewer ride-alongs with a particular supervisor. He worries the supervisor is building a case against him by nitpicking call times and write-ups.
workplace_relationships: Direct supervisor: Mark Delaney (source of harassment; makes derogatory remarks and threatens to cut hours)
Dispatcher: Jasmine Nguyen (generally fair; has hinted she has heard complaints from others)
Senior tech: Ron Castillo (advised him to "keep his head down" and avoid HR)
HR contact: Heather Sloane (seen as aligned with management; he is hesitant to report without legal guidance)

hobbies: Fishing from local piers and occasional trips to Fort De Soto
DIY home projects (fixing fans, replacing filters, small electrical)
Listening to talk radio and podcasts during commutes
habits: Checks work texts and schedules repeatedly due to anxiety about being singled out
Keeps a private log of incidents, dates, and witnesses at home
Evening walks when mood allows; otherwise tends to isolate and watch TV
Occasional increased alcohol use on high-stress nights (2–3 beers) to "wind down"
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with his spouse in a modest two-bedroom rental in the Tampa area; they renewed the lease recently but he is worried about stability if he loses hours or leaves the job during a legal dispute.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked meals (rice, vegetables, chicken/fish); tries to limit fast food during service calls but relies on convenience meals when stress is high. Drinks coffee daily and is trying to cut back in the afternoons to help sleep.
